title: Identity Match Response (Router → Publisher)
description: Router-to-publisher shape of the Identity Match response. The router collects each identity agent's ordered TMPX value chunks (see provider-identity-match-response.json) and forwards them under the emitting `provider_id`, preserving provider attribution and chunk order. TMPX chunks are opaque URL-safe strings — the publisher's deployment configuration (see publisher-tmpx-config.json) maps `(provider_id, ordinal)` to the local ad-server macro name, targeting key, VAST substitution, or play-log field. The provider→router boundary never carries a publisher-local destination name, so no cross-provider name-hijack surface exists. The serve_window_sec field defines a per-package single-shot fcap: after serving the user one impression on each eligible package, the publisher MUST re-query Identity Match before serving from those packages again. Extension fields (ext, context) are intentionally omitted to prevent data leakage across the identity privacy boundary.

request_id.description: Echoed request identifier from the identity match request

eligible_package_ids.description: Package IDs the user is eligible for. Packages not listed are ineligible.

serve_window_sec.description: Per-package single-shot fcap window, in seconds. After serving the user one impression on each eligible package within this window, the publisher MUST re-query Identity Match before serving from those packages again. This is NOT a router response cache TTL — it is a buyer-asserted serve throttle. Multi-impression frequency caps are handled separately by the buyer's impression tracker, which writes cap-fire events to the IdentityMatch cap-state store at the boundary regardless of this window. Maximum 300 — longer windows reduce IdentityMatch load but coarsen fcap granularity below what most campaigns require.

tmpx.description: DEPRECATED in favor of tmpx_providers. Routers MAY continue to populate this field for back-compat with consumers that only know the single-token shape; when both fields are present, tmpx_providers is authoritative. Single HPKE-encrypted exposure token containing the resolved user identity tokens. Wire format: kid.base64url_nopad(ciphertext) — unpadded base64url per RFC 4648 section 5 (no = characters). Publishers MUST treat this value as opaque pass-through data. Removed in 4.0.

tmpx_providers.description: Router-populated: ordered TMPX value chunks grouped by the originating identity provider's `provider_id`. Each entry's `values[]` is a copy of the provider's emitted `tmpx_values` list, in the same order. The publisher's deployment configuration (see publisher-tmpx-config.json) maps `(provider_id, index)` to the ad-server macro name, targeting key, VAST substitution, or play-log field for that surface — the protocol carries values and attribution only. Required by router conformance when any identity provider emitted TMPX in this request; collapsing per-provider tokens into a single string loses attribution and breaks per-provider impression accounting. Map keys MUST be valid provider_ids registered for this fan-out. Publishers MUST NOT parse, decode, or transform any element of `values[]` — each is an opaque URL-safe wire string substituted verbatim into the mapped destination.

values.description: Ordered TMPX value chunks for this provider. Position is the chunk's identity — the publisher's tmpx_macro_mapping resolves each ordinal to the local destination for this provider on this surface. Same URL-safe opaque wire strings the provider emitted, preserved in order. Capped at 2 chunks in v1; the cap MAY rise without a shape change.
